ucs.title: [LLM] Sidewinder (Rattlesnake) Office→mshta.exe remote HTA staging chain
ucs.description: Hunts Sidewinder's signature delivery chain: spearphishing attachment (RTF/DOCX exploiting CVE-2017-11882 Equation Editor or document with remote template injection) spawning mshta.exe to fetch a hosted .hta loader from an attacker-controlled domain (often typosquatting Pakistani/Nepali/Afghan/Chinese government entities). This bridge from Office to mshta with an HTTP(S) URL argument is highly characteristic of Sidewinder/T-APT-04 and is rare in benign environments.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_id) as pid values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=mshta.exe (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","explorer.exe") OR Processes.parent_process_name="wscript.exe") (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process="*javascript:*" OR Processes.process="*vbscript:*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "(?i)(?<staging_url>https?://[^\s\"']+)" | eval suspicious_tld=if(match(staging_url,"(?i)\.(xyz|top|info|live|cyou|site|online|click|gov\.[a-z]{2,3}\.)"),1,0) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","eqnedt32.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(https?://|javascript:|vbscript:|\.hta\b)"
| extend StagingUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend SuspiciousTld = iif(StagingUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(xyz|top|info|live|cyou|site|online|click)(/|$)", true, false)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.hta($|\?)"
    | project DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, StagingUrl, SuspiciousTld, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Sidewinder has used the Office→mshta→remote-HTA chain consistently across campaigns (ATT 2021, Securelist, Cyble reports), often paired with CVE-2017-11882 (eqnedt32.exe parent) and JavaScript/VBScript stagers fetched over HTTPS from short-lived lookalike domains. Generic admin use of mshta with a remote URL parented by Office or Equation Editor is essentially nonexistent outside of attacker activ

ucs.title: [LLM] Sidewinder LNK-delivered loader with Run-key persistence and DLL sideload
ucs.description: Hunts Sidewinder's post-exploitation pattern of dropping a renamed legitimate signed binary plus a malicious sideloaded DLL into a user-writable path (AppData / ProgramData / Public), then registering HKCU\...\Run persistence pointing at that binary. Sidewinder repeatedly uses this DLL search-order hijack (T1574.001) plus Registry Run-key (T1547.001) combination after initial LNK or HTA execution targeting South Asian government victims.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.exe") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | stats values(file_name) as files dc(file_name) as file_count by host user file_path | where file_count>=2 AND mvfilter(match(files,"(?i)\.dll$")) AND mvfilter(match(files,"(?i)\.exe$")) | join host user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*" Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*") by host Registry.user Registry.registry_value_data | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | rename user as user, registry_value_data as run_target] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let SidewinderPaths = pack_array(@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\");
let StagedDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where FolderPath has_any (SidewinderPaths)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".exe"
    | summarize Files=make_set(FileName), Folder=any(FolderPath), DropTime=min(Timestamp) by DeviceId, FolderPath
    | where array_length(Files) >= 2
      and tostring(Files) matches regex @"(?i)\.exe"
      and tostring(Files) matches regex @"(?i)\.dll";
let RunKey =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (SidewinderPaths)
    | project DeviceId, PersistTime=Timestamp, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData;
StagedDrops
| join kind=inner RunKey on DeviceId
| where PersistTime between (DropTime .. (DropTime + 1h))
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe")
    | project DeviceId, InitProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, InitCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ChildProc=FileName, ChildCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ProcTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| where ProcTime between ((DropTime - 10m) .. DropTime)
| project DropTime, DeviceId, FolderPath, Files, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData, InitProc, InitCmd, ChildProc, ChildCmd
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: Sidewinder/Rattlesnake (G0121) operations documented by AT&T, Securelist, and Cyble repeatedly stage a legitimate signed EXE plus a malicious DLL in user-writable directories and register HKCU Run-key persistence pointing at the renamed signed binary so the malicious DLL is loaded via search-order hijack on logon. Correlating co-located EXE+DLL drops in non-standard install paths with a near-simul
